hypothesis: Plain risk_on signals with no other sizing modifier and low regime_exit_score are not weak candidates; previous threshold narrowing proved those low-score winners should not be excluded, so they may deserve a larger non-stacking risk budget.
why_not_llm_soft_ranking: LLM soft-ranking remains high-upside but production-aligned replay samples are still too thin; this deterministic sizing sleeve is fully replayable and production-shared.

locked_variables: existing sector-specific sizing boosts and haircuts

market_regime_summary.late_strong: slow-melt bull / accepted-stack dominant tape with low-score plain risk_on winners
market_regime_summary.mid_weak: rotation-heavy bull with no affected low-score plain risk_on executed trades
market_regime_summary.old_thin: mixed-to-weak older tape where low-score plain risk_on exposure remained net-positive

pre_experiment_evidence.related_failure: exp-20260429-021 rejected regime_exit_score >= 0.08 eligibility gate because it removed useful low-score winners.

gate4_basis: Accepted: aggregate EV delta +0.1355 clears the +0.10 materiality bar, two of three fixed windows improved, no window regressed, trade count/win rate/survival were unchanged, and max drawdown expansion was limited to +0.04 pp in old_thin.

llm_metrics.blocker_relation: LLM soft-ranking is still deferred because sample coverage is insufficient; this experiment deliberately used deterministic replayable sizing instead.

risk_assessment.may_hurt: Low-score risk_on trades that are actually early tail-risk warnings could receive too much budget.
risk_assessment.mitigation: The rule applies only when the signal is already risk_on and has no existing sizing boost/haircut; missing regime_exit_score falls back to the accepted 1.25x baseline.

do_not_repeat_without_new_evidence: Nearby low-score multiplier tweaks above or below 1.5x
Stacking this rule on sector-specific boosts or risk haircuts
